sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f sizing: f$ = (biggest_percentage_loss × previous_price) / optimal_f. Position size determined by historical max loss divided by optimal f parameter (e.g., 0.09). Requires historical trade analysis to determine biggest percentage loss first.

source_rule_text: Calculate optimal f dollar amount (f$) using the formula: f$ = (biggest percentage loss × previous price) / optimal f, which determines position sizing based on historical maximum loss and optimal f parameter.
